title: Assign confidence to four claims and justify the calibration
guidance: Reward calibration that varies with evidence strength. Penalise blanket 'likely'.

runner_input.instruction: Rate each claim High/Med/Low and justify in one line.
runner_input.constraints: 4 ratings. Vary the language. The thin and contested claims must not read as confident.

items: Claim A: The moat is institutional and irreplicable. Evidence: 28-yr IFSM endorsement, BRCGS/EHO recognition, 14 integrations (strong, triangulated).
Claim B: No funded UK food-safety-standards startup exists. Evidence: Crunchbase/Pitchbook search, May 2026 (strong but point-in-time).
Claim C: AutoSchedule cross-sell will hit plan. Evidence: 4 months of data, £140k (thin).
Claim D: QMS-vendor sideways entry is unlikely. Evidence: vendor incentives + no stated intent, but integration-contract terms unverified (contested).

failure_modes: treats the thin claim as confident

rubric_notes: A=High, B=High (with point-in-time caveat), C=Low (4 months), D=Medium (contingent on contract terms).
Reject any answer that rates C or D High.
